motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: Clear and cold. The first real autumn sky: blue all the way down to the treeline, no cloud, no apology. The lake this morning was a mirror someone had laid flat on the earth and forgotten to collect.

undercurrent.note: The waterline has turned. The counting is, by Agnes's reckoning, complete—the lake found nine and was satisfied. Level is easing toward resolution; the urgency is gone and what remains is the domestic familiar. The hum has not stopped but it is no longer felt as pressure; it is felt as presence. The returned are gentle, the dusk gatherings are warm, the scarf stays in the lost-and-found. Tomorrow—Day 14, the coda—the town accepts and names its new normal. Watch for: Agnes giving the final word; the waterline continuing its quiet recession; whether the sign changes at all, or whether the town leaves it at seven forever as a private acknowledgment of what the lake counted and what it found.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: the lake wakes, goes silent, and begins to count the town
